{
  "analysis": "The data is now converted to fasttext format: `/app/train.ft` (650k lines, 460MB) and `/app/test.ft` (10k lines, 7.2MB). Labels are 0-4 balanced. I need to train a fasttext supervised model that achieves >=0.62 accuracy and is under 150MB. Model size is heavily affected by `dim` and `bucket` parameters, so I will use moderate values to stay under the size limit.",
  "plan": "I will train an initial fasttext supervised model with bigrams, dim=100, bucket=200000, epoch=10, lr=0.5, and evaluate on the provided test set. I will check accuracy and model size. If accuracy is insufficient, I will tune hyperparameters; if size is too large, I will reduce parameters or quantize.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 - <<'PY'
import fasttext, time, os

t0 = time.time()
model = fasttext.train_supervised(
    input='/app/train.ft',
    epoch=10,
    lr=0.5,
    wordNgrams=2,
    dim=100,
    bucket=200000,
    minCount=2,
    loss='softmax',
    thread=4,
    verbose=2,
)
print('train time', time.time()-t0)

# Evaluate
n, p, r = model.test('/app/test.ft')
print('test lines', n, 'precision', p, 'recall', r)

# Save and check size
model.save_model('/app/model.bin')
print('model size MB', os.path.getsize('/app/model.bin')/(1024*1024))
PY
",
      "duration": 30.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}
